title: Luria Delbruck– Ampicillin Plates
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6n9lp","text":"Adapted from Luria Delbruck protocol Spring2024 for E115L Evolution Lab class. Many populations of E. coli are grown in nutrient rich media then diluted and plated on ampicillin as selective environment at the MIC of this strain ~2 ug/mL. Expect many plates without CFUs and few plates with successful colonization.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"easu9","text":"LB media","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"507sl","text":"LB agar (2* 600 µL for 45 plates)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bso0r","text":"Ampicillin stock solution (100 mg/mL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d95qj","text":"45 Petri dishes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2m0f6","text":"96-well plate(s)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fna6v","text":"Plate sealing film","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"55c3n","text":"Microplate reader","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"64j5i","text":"Sterile glass beads","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8c4jm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"62l36","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: From a single colony, inoculate 2 mL of LB media and grow 24:00:00 at 35 °C
Using a plate reader, adjust OD620 to ~0.1 with LB
Dilute adjusted culture 10 -3 using LB media
Portion 198 µL LB media into first 4 rows of a 96-well plate
Inoculate 2 µL diluted culture into first four rows. Include a media control, e.g. columns 1-11 inoculated (purple), A12 media control (blue): LB only
Seal plate with clear plastic film and incubate 24:00:00 at 35 °C

For 44 2 µg/µL ampicillin plates, prepare 2 bottles of 600 mL LB agar. Remove 12 µL water before adding to LB agar powder. Autoclave using liquid settings at 121 °C with 00:15:00 sterilization time. Make 1 plate with normal LB agar (no antibiotics), prepare separate LB agar bottle. Place magnetic stir stick in bottles that will be used to make the antibiotic agar.
Pour plates into sterile petri dishes. Allow to cool to room temp then store at 4 °C
Inoculate 2 µL from original populations into the fresh LB (red). Do the same for media control (blue). LB only LB only
In a new 96-well plate or in the bottom 4 rows of the original 96-well plate, portion 198 µL of LB media

Plate 50 µL of diluted culture on LB agar (no antibiotics) using sterile glass beads. Incubate 24:00:00 at 35 °C and record number of CFUs Shooting for 50-200 CFUs per 50 µL (such that there are 10-50 cells per 2 µL ). Adjust dilution factor as needed.

After media is cool enough to touch the bottle with back of your hand without pain, add 12 µL of 100 mg/mL ampicillin to each bottle. Use stir plate to mix for 00:05:00 . In working with antibiotics, use extra precaution and ensure proper PPE. After mixing in ampicillin, pour plates in a timely manner. Agar will solidify and magnetic stir bar cannot be microwaved/ antibiotics cannot be reheated.
Plate 50 µL from each population (red wells) onto ampicillin agar plate. Shake with sterile glass beads. Do the same for media control (blue well).
Incubate inoculated agar plates for 24:00:00 at 35 °C
creator: Jaycee Fahrner
